terms.definition: An on-chain governance item with a title, description, quorum requirement, and fixed voting window. Any address may create one.
The minimum total votes (yes + no + abstain) required for a proposal to be valid. Set by the proposer at creation time.
The number of governance tokens held by a voter at the time of voting. More tokens = more influence.
A vote choice that counts toward quorum but neither supports nor opposes the proposal.
The act of closing a proposal after its voting period expires. Anyone can trigger this. The contract then marks the proposal Passed or Rejected.
Admin action that marks a Passed proposal as Executed, signalling that its outcome has been acted on off-chain or on-chain.
Admin action that stops an Active proposal before its voting window ends.
The sequence of states a proposal moves through: Active → Passed/Rejected → Executed, or Active → Cancelled.
